Title: Phototropin connects blue light perception to starch metabolism in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon and energy storage reserve; starch sheaths around the pyrenoid help reduce CO2 leakage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses starch accumulation under blue/white light; loss of PHOT causes starch hyperaccumulation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Phototropin blue-light photoreceptor kinase with LOV photosensory domains and serine/threonine kinase domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally active under white and blue light; inactive under red light
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Chloroplast, around pyrenoid as sheaths and in chloroplast stroma under high accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phot mutant phenotyping; complementation with WT PHOT; kinase-domain-only active construct; dead-kinase and LOV-only constructs; starch quantification; RT-qPCR of GAP1; TEM
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue, white, and red light; continuous light; 12 h light/12 h dark cycles
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHOT is the blue-light receptor required to repress starch accumulation in Chlamydomonas. 2. phot mutants accumulate about threefold more starch, and complementation with WT PHOT or constitutively active kinase rescues the phenotype. 3. PHOT kinase activity is required for suppression of both GAP1 mRNA and starch accumulation. 4. PHOT promotes blue-light-dependent dephosphorylation of PMSK1, placing PHOT upstream of the PMSK1–GAP1 module.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cryptochrome mutants (aCRY, pCRY, acrypcry) did not alter starch accumulation, supporting PHOT specificity. Authors also infer additional blue-light-responsive regulators may exist beyond PHOT-PMSK1.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Under the tested low-light conditions, elevated starch in phot did not measurably affect growth, photosynthesis, total protein, lipid content, or lipid composition
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating PHOT signaling may tune carbon allocation toward starch in microalgae without major growth penalty under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics: PRIDE PXD046943; Phosphoproteomics: PRIDE PXD045599

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon and energy storage reserve; pyrenoid starch sheath helps limit CO2 leakage
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Calvin-Benson cycle / carbon fixation linked precursor supply for starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Chloroplast carbon assimilation and phosphorylated sugar precursor supply to starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the interconversion of glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate and 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate in the chloroplast Calvin-Benson cycle, contributing phosphorylated sugar intermediates that support starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for the studied phenotype, but not explicitly established as a canonical rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation; overexpression increases starch, while knockdown in phot reduces starch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly diurnally regulated; low after light onset, maximal near end of light phase; much higher under red light or in phot mutant
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Chloroplast, especially pyrenoid sheath and chloroplast stroma
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Whole-cell quantitative proteomics; RT-qPCR; transgenic overexpression lines; amiRNA knockdown in phot; starch measurements
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue, white, and red light; diurnal 12 h light/12 h dark cycles
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAP1 protein was 27.5-fold more abundant in phot than WT and GAP1 mRNA was strongly elevated in phot. 2. GAP1 expression is repressed by blue light through PHOT. 3. GAP1 overexpression in WT increased starch accumulation, whereas GAP1 knockdown in phot reduced starch. 4. GAP1 is a major positive determinant of PHOT-dependent starch metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors describe GAP1 as a Calvin-Benson cycle enzyme and an enhancer of starch metabolism through generation of phosphorylated sugars as precursors of starch synthesis; effect on starch is dosage-dependent because modest overexpression did not increase starch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upregulating GAP1 can increase starch accumulation, but strong induction appears more effective than modest overexpression and may need coupling with upstream signaling control

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon and energy storage reserve; pyrenoid starch sheath helps reduce CO2 leakage
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ser/Thr protein kinase; PMSK-like family; kinase domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Phosphorylation-state-dependent positive regulation of GAP1 mRNA accumulation; active phosphorylated S120 form promotes downstream response
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of carbon metabolism/starch biosynthesis via GAP1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Phosphorylated PMSK1 activates/promotes GAP1 mRNA accumulation; unphosphorylated PMSK1 fails to promote GAP1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Blue light perceived by PHOT; red light favors phosphorylated/active PMSK1 state; possible additional circadian/diurnal/photosynthetic inputs to GAP1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major transducer linking blue-light perception by PHOT to starch accumulation; S120 phosphorylation status determines whether starch-promoting GAP1 is induced
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Phosphorylated PMSK1 promotes GAP1 expression and starch accumulation; pmsk1 knockout lowers GAP1 and starch; phosphomimetic S120D elevates both
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional state depends on phosphorylation rather than reported expression dynamics
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phosphoproteomics; FLAG-tagged Phos-tag immunoblotting; phosphomimetic/non-phosphorylatable mutants (S120D/S120A); kinase-dead mutant (D442N); CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; double mutant with phot; RT-qPCR; starch profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue versus red light after dark acclimation; continuous and 12 h light/12 h dark conditions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PMSK1 S120 is highly phosphorylated in dark and becomes dephosphorylated after blue light in WT but not in phot. 2. Blue-light-activated PHOT is required for PMSK1 dephosphorylation. 3. PMSK1S120D increases GAP1 mRNA and starch, whereas PMSK1S120A keeps both low. 4. PMSK1 kinase activity is necessary for this regulatory effect. 5. CRISPR loss of PMSK1 lowers GAP1 expression and starch accumulation, establishing PMSK1 as a key regulator downstream of PHOT.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose PMSK1-P as the active form; PHOT likely promotes its dephosphorylation, possibly via an unknown phosphatase. pmsk1 does not fully abolish light-quality effects on GAP1 unless combined with phot, implying additional regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Precise kinase engineering of PMSK1, especially phosphostate manipulation at S120, is proposed as a strategy to enhance starch production in green microalgae
